audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Ten Words, liberation preface, Sabbath, honor parents, social prohibitions, theophany fear, altar instruction.

verses.restored: Divine spoke all these words, saying,
I am YHWH your Divine, who brought you out from land of Egypt, from house of servants/slaves.
You shall have no other gods before my face.
You shall not make for yourself a carved image or any form that is in heavens above, on earth beneath, or in waters under earth.
You shall not bow down to them and shall not serve them, for I, YHWH your Divine, am a jealous Divine, visiting iniquity of fathers upon children to third and fourth of those hating me,
but doing loyal love to thousands of those loving me and keeping my commands.
You shall not lift/carry name of YHWH your Divine for emptiness, for YHWH will not clear the one who carries his name for emptiness.
Remember the Sabbath day, to sanctify it.
Six days you shall serve/work and do all your work,
but seventh day is Sabbath to YHWH your Divine; you shall not do any work: you, your son, your daughter, your male servant, your female servant, your beast, and your sojourner within your gates.
For six days YHWH made heavens and earth, sea, and all that is in them, and rested on seventh day; therefore YHWH blessed Sabbath day and sanctified it.
Honor your father and your mother, so that your days may be long upon the ground that YHWH your Divine gives you.
You shall not murder.
You shall not commit adultery.
You shall not steal.
You shall not answer against your companion as false witness.
You shall not covet your companion's house; you shall not covet your companion's wife, male servant, female servant, ox, donkey, or anything that belongs to your companion.
All the people saw the voices/thunders, flashes, voice of horn, and mountain smoking; the people saw and trembled and stood from afar.
They said to Moses, You speak with us, and we will listen; but let not Divine speak with us, lest we die.
Moses said to the people, Do not fear, for the Divine has come in order to test you, and so that fear/reverence of him may be before your faces, so you do not sin/misalign.
The people stood from afar, and Moses approached the thick darkness where the Divine was.
YHWH said to Moses, Thus you shall say to children of Israel: You yourselves saw that from heavens I spoke with you.
You shall not make alongside me gods of silver, and gods of gold you shall not make for yourselves.

An altar of earth you shall make for me and sacrifice upon it your burnt offerings and peace offerings, your flock and herd; in every place where I cause my name to be remembered, I will come to you and bless you.
If you make for me an altar of stones, you shall not build them cut, for if you swing your iron tool upon it, you profane it.
You shall not go up by steps to my altar, so that your nakedness not be uncovered upon it.

translation_notes: The Ten Words begin with liberation: YHWH brought you out from the house of slaves before any command is given.
Sabbath protects servants, animals, and sojourners; rest is social liberation practice.
Do not carry the name for emptiness includes misuse of Divine name for false, vain, manipulative, or unjust purposes.
Coveting list reflects ancient household/property ordering and requires later ethical audit; preserve source wording while guarding against treating people as property in application.
Simple earth/uncut-stone altar resists monumental control and technical domination of worship.
safety_note: Exodus 20 should not be framed as abstract legalism or coercive religion. It is liberation-shaped covenant speech that protects worship, rest, family continuity, life, fidelity, property, testimony, and desire from domination.
